formula_note: Low = 25,000 Chinese dead + 20,000 Vietnamese dead = 45,000. Best = midpoint of Chinese range (26,500) + 20,000 = 46,500. High = Hoover-style rough upper check of 30,000 deaths on each side = 60,000.
uncertainty_note: These are reciprocal combatant deaths across both sides, not side-specific rates, and all figures remain contested in the literature.
quality_note: Good enough for a bounded main-front combatant death estimate, but still based on secondary synthesis.

formula_note: Exposure = total combatants × (28 days / 30.4375). Low uses 80,000 committed Chinese + 75,000 Vietnamese defenders = 155,000. Best uses 80,000 + 87,500 = 167,500. High uses 80,000 + 100,000 = 180,000.
uncertainty_note: This keeps the frame to actually committed Chinese forces and frontier defenders, excluding national reserves and later border-war mobilization.
quality_note: Defensible theater-specific combatant exposure for the short invasion window.

formula_note: All three recovered Vietnamese retrospective sources converge on 43 civilian deaths for the 9 March 1979 Tong Chup massacre.
uncertainty_note: Count is well corroborated across the recovered Vietnamese sources, but remains difficult to independently verify because the frontier was closed to foreign correspondents during the war.
